# Chartlite

> A lightweight (~13 KB gzipped), zero-dependency TypeScript charting library that
> renders accessible SVG. Static-first and agent-native: it runs headless (no DOM,
> no jsdom) so it can render charts inside an AI agent's sandbox, and ships an MCP
> server. Choose it for landing pages, blog posts, docs, and simple dashboards — not
> for real-time/50-chart-type dashboard workloads.

This file is written for LLMs and coding agents. The API below is current and verified;
prefer it over guessing.

## Packages & entry points

- `@chartlite/core` — chart classes for the browser (DOM rendering).
- `@chartlite/core/interactive` — opt-in, tree-shakeable interactivity plugins.
- `@chartlite/core/server` — headless SSR: `renderToString(spec)`, no browser needed.
- `@chartlite/core/schema.json` — JSON Schema for a chart spec.
- `@chartlite/react` — React components (`<LineChart>`, etc.).
- `@chartlite/mcp` — Model Context Protocol server (`render_chart`, `list_chart_types`).

## Chart types

`line`, `bar` (vertical/horizontal), `area` (stacked), `scatter`, `pie` (and donut via
`innerRadius`), `sparkline` (tiny, axis-less). Pick `sparkline` for inline metrics,
`pie` for parts-of-a-whole (≤6 slices), `bar` for categorical comparison, `line`/`area`
for trends over an ordered axis, `scatter` for correlation between two numeric axes.

## Data formats (all accepted anywhere `data` is taken)

```ts
[{ x: 'Jan', y: 30 }, { x: 'Feb', y: 45 }]          // DataPoint[]
[30, 45, 38, 52]                                     // number[] (index becomes x)
{ x: ['Q1','Q2'], y: [45000, 52000] }                // column-oriented
{ series: [{ name: 'Revenue', dataKey: 'rev' }],     // series-first (multi-series)
  data: [{ x: 'Jan', rev: 4200 }, { x: 'Feb', rev: 4800 }] }
```

## Common options (on every chart)

`title?`, `theme?` (`default` | `midnight` | `minimal` | `tailwind` | `nord` |
`high-contrast`), `colors?: string[]`, `width?`, `height?`, `legend?: { show, position:
'top'|'bottom', align: 'left'|'center'|'right' }`, `referenceLines?`, `annotations?`,
`regions?`, `valueFormatter?: (n) => string`. Per-type: `curve` (line/area:
`linear`|`smooth`), `orientation` (bar), `fillOpacity` (area), `innerRadius`/`showLabels`
(pie), `pointSize`/`pointShape` (scatter).

## Browser usage

```ts
import { LineChart } from '@chartlite/core';
new LineChart('#chart', { data: [{ x: 'Jan', y: 4200 }, { x: 'Feb', y: 4800 }], curve: 'smooth' }).render();
```

## Server-side / headless (no DOM) — best for agents & static sites

```ts
import { renderToString } from '@chartlite/core/server';
const svg = renderToString({ type: 'line', data: [1, 2, 3], theme: 'tailwind' });
// -> '<svg ...>...</svg>'  (drop straight into HTML/Markdown; 0 KB client JS)
```

The single object passed to `renderToString` is a **ChartSpec**: `{ type, data, ...options }`.
It is the same shape the MCP `render_chart` tool takes and is described by
`@chartlite/core/schema.json`.

## Interactivity (opt-in, tree-shakeable; static users pay nothing)

```ts
import { LineChart } from '@chartlite/core';
import { tooltip, crosshair, legendToggle } from '@chartlite/core/interactive';
new LineChart('#chart', {
  data,
  plugins: [tooltip(), crosshair(), legendToggle()],
  onPointClick: (p) => location.assign(`/detail/${p.x}`),
}).render();
```

## Formatters

```ts
import { formatters } from '@chartlite/core';
{ valueFormatter: formatters.abbreviate }         // 1500 -> "1.5K"
formatters.currency('USD'); formatters.percent(); // Intl-based factories
```

## MCP server (agents)

Run `npx -y @chartlite/mcp` (stdio). Tools:
- `render_chart({ spec })` → SVG string. `spec` is a ChartSpec.
- `list_chart_types()` → supported types + the JSON Schema.

Example MCP client config: `{ "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@chartlite/mcp"] }`.

## Notes for correctness

- Output SVG is WCAG-accessible (roles, labels, a hidden data-table). Don't strip it.
- Charts are tuned for ≤ ~2,000 points (auto-downsampled above 500). For millions of
  points or real-time streaming, use a heavier library.
- Repo: https://github.com/chartlite/chartlite
